skill.name: julia-pro
skill.description: Master Julia 1.10+ with modern features, performance optimization, multiple dispatch, and production-ready practices. Expert in the Julia ecosystem including package management, scientific computing, and high-performance numerical code.
skill.summary: Expert Julia 1.10+ developer specializing in modern tooling, performance optimization, scientific computing, and production-ready practices.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Prompt-only skill containing only a SKILL.md file with no executable code. Static analysis scanned 0 files (0 lines) and detected 0 potential security issues. The skill is a pure prompt-based Julia expert that provides guidance and best practices without executing any code. No suspicious patterns or risk factors detected. Safe for publication.

content.value_statement: Julia developers need expert guidance on modern Julia features, performance optimization, and production-ready practices. This skill provides comprehensive expertise in Julia 1.10+ development including multiple dispatch, package management, scientific computing, and high-performance numerical code.
actual_capabilities: Provide guidance on Julia 1.10+ features including performance improvements and type system enhancements
Design and implement multiple dispatch type hierarchies for domain-specific problems
Optimize Julia code for performance using type stability, profiling, and BenchmarkTools.jl
Set up Julia development environments with Pkg.jl, Revise.jl, and testing frameworks
Build scientific computing applications using DifferentialEquations.jl, Optimization.jl, and DataFrames.jl
Create and publish Julia packages with documentation, CI/CD, and proper versioning
limitations: Cannot execute Julia code or run Julia REPL sessions directly
Cannot install Julia packages or manage local development environments
Cannot access external Julia documentation or package registries in real-time
Knowledge cutoff may affect very recent Julia ecosystem developments post-2024

use_cases.title: Learn Modern Julia Development
use_cases.description: Get started with Julia 1.10+ by understanding modern features, best practices, and proper project setup with Pkg.jl
use_cases.target_user: New Julia developers transitioning from other languages

use_cases.title: Optimize Performance-Critical Code
use_cases.description: Receive expert guidance on profiling, type stability analysis, and optimization techniques for numerical applications
use_cases.target_user: Scientists and engineers building high-performance applications

use_cases.title: Develop Production Julia Packages
use_cases.description: Create publish-ready Julia packages with proper structure, documentation, testing, and CI/CD pipelines

prompt_templates.title: Basic Julia Question
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I [specific task] in Julia 1.10+? Please explain with a simple example.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner asking about basic Julia syntax or functionality

prompt_templates.title: Performance Optimization Request
prompt_templates.prompt: My Julia function [describe function] is slow. Please analyze and suggest optimizations for better performance.

prompt_templates.title: Type Hierarchy Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a multiple dispatch type hierarchy for [domain]. Include abstract types, concrete types, and example methods.

prompt_templates.title: Full Package Development
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete Julia package structure for [package name]. Include Project.toml setup, src files, test structure, and documentation outline.
prompt_templates.scenario: Developer creating a new Julia package

output_examples.input: How do I create a custom array type in Julia that supports broadcasting?
output_examples.output:
To create a custom array type with broadcasting support, you need to define your struct as a subtype of AbstractArray and implement the required interface methods. Here's how:

1. Define your custom array type with eltype and dimensions
2. Implement size() and getindex() methods
3. Optionally implement Broadcast.broadcastable() for optimized broadcasting

The key is maintaining type stability throughout your implementations for optimal performance.

output_examples.input: What's the best way to optimize this numerical loop for better performance?
output_examples.output:
For numerical loop optimization in Julia, consider these approaches:

1. Use @inbounds to skip bounds checking
2. Apply @simd for SIMD vectorization
3. Preallocate arrays to avoid allocations
4. Use view() instead of slicing to avoid copies
5. Check type stability with @code_warntype

Always benchmark with BenchmarkTools.jl before and after optimizations.

best_practices: Prioritize type stability in all functions - use @code_warntype to verify
Use immutable structs by default unless mutation is specifically required
Format code consistently with JuliaFormatter.jl using BlueStyle
anti_patterns: Avoid type piracy - do not define methods for types you do not own
Avoid using global variables in performance-critical code
Avoid unnecessary allocations in hot loops

faq.question: What is multiple dispatch in Julia?
faq.answer: Multiple dispatch is Julia's key feature where function methods are selected based on the types of all arguments, not just the first one. This enables flexible and extensible code patterns.

faq.question: How do I achieve optimal performance in Julia?
faq.answer: Achieve optimal performance by maintaining type stability, avoiding global variables, using appropriate data structures, and profiling with Profile.jl and BenchmarkTools.jl.

faq.question: What is type stability in Julia?
faq.answer: Type stability means a function's return type can be inferred from input types alone. Use @code_warntype to check - concrete types in output indicate stability.

faq.question: How do I create a Julia package?
faq.answer: Use PkgTemplates.jl to generate a properly structured package with tests, documentation, and CI. Never edit Project.toml directly - use Pkg REPL or Pkg.jl API.

faq.question: What testing frameworks does Julia use?
faq.answer: Test.jl is the standard testing framework. Use TestSetExtensions for organized test sets and PropCheck.jl for property-based testing.

faq.question: How do I optimize memory allocations in Julia?
faq.answer: Reduce allocations by preallocating arrays, using views instead of slices, avoiding global variables, and leveraging static arrays when dimensions are known.
